era_context: Egypt's Old Kingdom, Dynasty IV. Reign of Khufu (c. 2589–2566 BCE) or transition toward Khafre (c. 2558–2532 BCE). Mesopotamia: Akkad Empire under Sargon of Akkad (r. c. 2334–2279 BCE — note: this is later than 2546 BCE, so Akkad was not yet dominant; instead, Sumerian city-states and Early Dynastic III period were active). Indus Valley: Harappan urban centres flourishing. This is the height of Old Kingdom Egypt.
geographic_coverage_gaps: Mesopotamia, Indus Valley, Sub-Saharan Africa, East Asia, Americas — no year-specific records
disconfirming_evidence: No specific events for 2546 BCE are attested in surviving sources. The year falls within the Old Kingdom period, but no year-specific documentation exists.
historiographic_note: The historical record for 2546 BCE is negligible. No contemporary or near-contemporary sources document specific events for this year. The period is known only through monumental and archaeological evidence, which does not provide year-by-year chronology. Absolute dating for the Old Kingdom is uncertain by ±50–100 years.
